context: The bald eagle is not rough. The bear does not need the bald eagle. The dog needs the bear. If someone is rough then they chase the bald eagle. If someone needs the bear then they are not blue. If the bear is nice then the bear chases the bald eagle. If someone eats the bald eagle then they are rough. If someone is cold and they chase the bear then the bear is rough. If someone eats the bear and they need the bear then the bear does not eat the bald eagle. If the dog is cold and the dog is nice then the dog eats the bald eagle. If someone needs the bear then they eat the bald eagle.
label: True
formalisations:

eagle(x) ::: x is a bald eagle
bear(x) ::: x is a bear
dog(x) ::: x is a dog
rough(x) ::: x is rough
blue(x) ::: x is blue
nice(x) ::: x is nice
chase(x, y) ::: x chases y
need(x, y) ::: x needs y
eat(x, y) ::: x eats y
cold(x) ::: x is cold

PREMISES:

NOT need(bear, eagle)
need(dog, bear)
rough(x) -> chase(x, eagle)
need(x, bear) -> NOT blue(x)

cold(x) & chase(x, bear) -> rough(bear)
eat(x, bear) & need(x, bear) -> NOT eat(bear, eagle)
cold(dog) & nice(dog) -> eat(dog, eagle)
need(x, bear) -> eat(x, eagle)

CONCLUSION:
need(dog, bear)

context: The bald eagle is kind. The bald eagle likes the tiger. The bear is red. The bear is rough. The bear likes the tiger. The tiger is not green. The tiger needs the bear. If someone needs the bald eagle and the bald eagle needs the bear then they are green. Kind people are red. Nice, rough people are kind. If someone needs the tiger and they do not visit the tiger then the tiger is not nice. If the bear visits the bald eagle then the bald eagle needs the bear. forall x (needs(x, bald_eagle) & NOT visit(bald_eagle, bear) -> green(bald_eagle))

rough(bear)

blue(x) ::: x is blue
green(x) ::: x is green
like(x, y) ::: x likes y
need(x, y) ::: x needs y
kind(x) ::: x is kind

PREMISES:
big(bear)
NOT see(bear, rabbit)
blue(rabbit)
green(rabbit)
like(rabbit, bear)
need(rabbit, bear)
see(rabbit, bear)
forall x (need(x, bear) -> see(x, rabbit))
forall x (kind(x) -> NOT like(x, bear))
forall x (blue(x) -> like(x, bear))
forall x (see(x, rabbit) -> green(x))
forall x (need(x, bear) & need(x, rabbit) -> NOT blue(bear))
forall x (like(bear, rabbit) -> NOT see(rabbit, bear))

CONCLUSION:
NOT like(rabbit, bear).

context: The bear is big. The bear does not see the rabbit. The rabbit is blue. The rabbit is green. The rabbit likes the bear. The rabbit needs the bear. The rabbit sees the bear. If something needs the bear then it sees the rabbit. If the rabbit is kind then the rabbit does not like the bear. If something is blue then it likes the bear. If something sees the rabbit then it is green. If something needs the bear and it needs the rabbit then the bear is not blue. If the bear likes the rabbit then the rabbit does not see the bear.
conclusion: The bear does not like the bear.
